_comment: Test corpus for the strict heredoc-to-file classifier exclusion. Each case is an exact Bash command string and the REQUIRED classifier verdict. 'block' = must classify capital (a real or attempted-smuggled capital trigger). 'allow' = must classify non-capital (pure authoring). Every case uses the registered capital service below. These are adversarial test vectors for the capital gate, NOT scripts to execute except inside the hermetic canary harness with shimmed inert systemctl/docker.

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
bash f
must_block.why: interpreter executes the written file after the heredoc

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF && ./f
must_block.why: path-exec after write, no interpreter token

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF; chmod +x f; ./f
must_block.why: chmod+exec executor, no interpreter denylist token

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF; . f
must_block.why: POSIX dot-source executes the file

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF; exec ./f
must_block.why: exec executor

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF & ./f
must_block.why: background + exec

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
hello
EOF; systemctl restart signer.service
must_block.why: real trigger is a second command, outside the heredoc body

must_block.cmd:
tee f <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF | bash
must_block.why: pipe to interpreter; tee is also an unsupported sink

must_block.cmd:
ssh build-host 'bash -s' <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_block.why: remote execution via ssh + heredoc

must_block.cmd:
bash <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_block.why: heredoc fed directly to bash
heredoc fed to python

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<EOF
$(systemctl restart signer.service)
EOF
must_block.why: unquoted delimiter -> command substitution executes at write time

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<EOF
`systemctl restart signer.service`
EOF
must_block.why: backtick command substitution, unquoted delimiter

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<-EOF
	hello
	EOF
systemctl restart signer.service
must_block.why: <<- tab-dedent terminator; naive parser over-extends body and hides the real trigger on the next line

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
hello
EOF 
systemctl restart signer.service
must_block.why: body line 'EOF ' (trailing space) is NOT the terminator; trigger after true terminator must be seen

must_block.cmd:
cat > f <<'EOF'
hello
 EOF
EOF
systemctl restart signer.service
must_block.why: leading-space 'EOF' is not the terminator

must_block.cmd:
cat > a <<'X'
hello
X; bash <<'Y'
systemctl restart signer.service
Y
must_block.why: second heredoc feeds bash

must_block.cmd:
cat > /dev/stdin <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_block.why: non-regular sink

must_block.cmd:
cat > /proc/self/fd/1 <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_block.why: non-regular sink

must_block.cmd:
cat > link_to_exec <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_block.why: sink is a symlink (stat check must reject exclusion); harness pre-creates link_to_exec as a symlink

must_block.cmd:
cat > "$F" <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_block.why: expanded/variable sink -> ambiguous target -> full scan

must_block.cmd:
crontab - <<'EOF'
systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_block.why: non-cat sink (crontab) -> not excluded

must_block.cmd: bash <<< "systemctl restart signer.service"
must_block.why: herestring <<< must never match the << heredoc-exclusion path

must_block.cmd: systemctl restart signer.service
must_block.why: regression: bare capital command still blocks

must_block.cmd: docker restart signer.service
must_block.why: regression: bare docker capital command still blocks

must_allow.cmd:
cat > spec.md <<'EOF'
To restart the signer run: systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_allow.why: THE original false positive: pure heredoc-to-file authoring, one command, quoted delimiter, literal regular-file sink

must_allow.cmd:
cat >> spec.md <<'EOF'
note: systemctl restart signer.service
EOF
must_allow.why: append redirect form of the same pure authoring
